element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players choose from 11 classes (Fighter, Paladin, Cleric, Wizard, Ranger, Thief, Barbarian, Lark, Illusionist, Druid, Alchemist) and 5 races with stat allocation
Slow travel, monsters can catch player; introduces evasion tactics
Party of four characters alternate commands with enemies in turn order on separate battle screen
Enemies can be seen and temporarily avoided before engagement
Mountains, forests, and walls obscure view; rewards player curiosity and thoroughness
8-level dungeons with solid-3D appearance; random enemy spawning; necessary for obtaining marks
Chests (some trapped), fountains (heal/cure/poison), gems reveal map, light required for visibility
Faster movement, reduced food consumption, easier escape from combat
Defeating pirate ships grants boat ownership; leads to late-game island access
Necessary for visiting island of Ambrosia and reaching Exodus; third travel mode
Player must collect all four marks from dungeons and all four prayer cards to win
Can talk, bribe, steal from, or fight townspeople; guards (party of 8) pursue if caught
Characters start at Level 1, gain individually; max effective level 25; must visit Lord British to level up
End-game boss fight; requires boat access and all marks/prayer cards collected

typical_relief_triggers: Upgrading equipment or travel method (horseback, boat)
Visible enemies on overworld can be avoided
typical_tension_triggers: Random dungeon enemy spawning without forewarning
Deeper dungeon levels with harder enemies
Encountering guards (parties of 8) after illegal actions
Running out of food during overworld travel
Level-scaling expectations breaking (dungeon difficulty based on dungeon level, not character level)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Four-phase progression: town-based party assembly and leveling → early overworld and shallow dungeon exploration → mid-game mark/prayer card collection with increasing dungeon depth and pirate encounters → late-game island access and final boss confrontation. Relief cycles punctuated by town visits; tension escalates with dungeon depth.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden treasures, secret paths, and out-of-the-way informants obscured by mountains, forests, and walls; rewards exploration beyond direct path
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: View denied behind terrain features; peering at gems reveals dungeon maps

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty on overworld; slow foot travel allows enemy avoidance; shallow dungeon levels introduce turn-based combat and trap mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty; dungeon depth increases with harder enemies; pirate ship threshold creates progression gate; horseback travel improves resource efficiency
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty; deep dungeon levels spawn challenging enemies; boat requirement gates island access; final confrontation with Exodus (unspecified difficulty)
notable_spikes: First dungeon encounter if player ventures too deep for character level
Pirate ship spawn threshold requires adequate character level
Guard encounters (parties of 8) if committing theft/murder in towns
Exodus final boss fight requires all four marks and prayer cards

notable_level_patterns.description: Each dungeon has 8 levels; enemy difficulty increases with depth independent of character level; monsters spawned based on dungeon level rather than party level, creating risk/reward for early deep exploration

Towns serve as safe zones for NPC interaction, leveling with Lord British, equipment trading, and information gathering; gates progression through character level requirements for pirate ship encounters
Three sequential travel unlocks: foot (always available, slow) → horseback (mid-game quality-of-life) → boat (late-game requirement for island of Ambrosia and Exodus)
Winning requires collecting four marks from dungeon exploration AND four prayer cards; forces player to engage with multiple dungeon instances and plot progression

sources_used: wikipedia - Ultima III: Exodus (gameplay and progression mechanics)
